intro: The most concrete split between Paddle and Maxio is the pricing model: Paddle runs on usage-based pricing from 5% + $0.50/transaction, while Maxio uses subscription from $100. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, Paddle carries high lock-in (high — mor model = paddle handles everything, fromwith adapts under paddle) and usage-based pricing from 5% + $0.50/transaction; Maxio carries medium lock-in (billing data exportable) and subscription from $100. Paddle is a fit when you are saas, b2b. Maxio is a fit when you are saas. The honest trade-off: Paddle is explicitly not for marketplace/platform payments; Maxio is explicitly not for simple projects. On the product side, Paddle is described as Merchant of Record and subscription billing platform for B2B/B2C SaaS with global tax, checkout, and revenue recovery, and Maxio is described as Subscription and revenue management platform for B2B SaaS combining billing, revenue recognition, and financial metrics.
quick_take: Paddle is for saas, b2b; Maxio is for saas; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Paddle if its usage pricing model fits your budget shape better than Maxio's subscription model, and you are saas, b2b.
verdict_b: Choose Maxio if its subscription pricing model fits your budget shape better than Paddle's usage model, and you are saas.

faq.a: Paddle starts at 5% + $0.50/transaction; Maxio starts at $100. On entry price alone that is the comparison, but real cost scales with users, events or volume, which the entry prices do not capture. Check each vendor's full tier list before committing.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Paddle to Maxio?
faq.a: Migration from Paddle is harder: Paddle is rated high lock-in (high — mor model = paddle handles everything, fromwith adapts under paddle). You can still move, but expect schema-mapping, export tooling and a longer cutover window. Budget engineering time accordingly.

On our scoring Paddle rates 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Maxio. That suggests day-to-day workflows (CLI, SDKs, docs) are smoother in Paddle. Scores are directional; run a short spike on your own stack to confirm.

faq.q: Is Maxio a good alternative to Paddle?
faq.a: Maxio is pitched at saas, which overlaps with Paddle's fit (saas, b2b) but is not identical. Note that Maxio is explicitly not for simple projects, so it only replaces Paddle if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (high vs medium), which is the main thing to weigh.
